original_hypothesis.title: Admin freeze locks sellers' listed vestings with no escape hatch (unlist also blocked)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The isFreeze modifier blocks both listVesting and unlistVesting when the marketplace is frozen. If admin freezes during an incident (or maliciously), sellers cannot unlist to recover their vesting allocations from the VestingManager escrow. This can lock assets indefinitely until unfreeze.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is an operationally important incident-mode failure: emergency freeze should typically allow exits. The code currently prevents any exit, creating a single-point-of-failure on admin action and raising incident recovery risk.

proof_of_signal.pattern: modifier isFreeze requires !isMarketplaceFreeze; applied to unlistVesting() and listVesting()/spotPurchase()

preconditions: Seller has an active listing with vesting allocation escrowed in VestingManager
Seller attempts to call unlistVesting to retrieve their allocation

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Freeze disables both entry and exit paths for listings
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires admin to setMarketplaceStatus(true) or keep it true

operational_questions: Is there an intended emergency withdrawal path outside Marketplace.unlistVesting?
Can VestingManager or StepVesting provide a recovery method for escrowed allocations? (Not shown.)
What operational policy governs freezing and unfreezing?
validation_requirements: State test: create listing, freeze marketplace, confirm unlistVesting reverts
Assess whether admin can bypass isFreeze through any other function path (should not)

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Wait until many sellers have listed allocations (escrowed)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Admin freezes marketplace (or compromised admin does so)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Sellers cannot unlist; allocations remain in VestingManager address(this)
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Requires privileged actor or compromise (out-of-scope for HIGH realism)

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: None required if legitimate incident freeze still causes lock
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Mass asset lock of listed vesting allocations; marketplace unusable
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High if admin unavailable or refuses to unfreeze
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All listing lifecycle operations during freeze

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: setMarketplaceStatus(true) followed by unlistVesting revert spikes
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Large accumulation of vesting allocations held by VestingManager without outflows
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Freeze toggles without corresponding incident communications

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
